faculty: Faculty of Health and Life Sciences
overview: Bachelor of Public Health (BPH) is an undergraduate degree program focusing on promoting and protecting the health and well-being of communities and populations. The field of public health is multidisciplinary, drawing from various disciplines such as epidemiology, biostatistics, environmental health, social and behavioral sciences, health policy, and management. The BPH program provides a comprehensive understanding of these disciplines and their application to public health practice with the hospital visits, thesis projects and internships.
waiver_info: Result-based waiver (Bachelor's), Waiver on SGPA (Semester result), Female quota, Need-based, Player quota, Physically challenged/specially-abled, tribal/ethnic group quota, Sibling/spouse and son/daughter of current students quota, Waiver for DIU alumni, Group admission quota, Freedom fighter quota
eligibility: SSC/Dakhil, HSC/Alim: Students having minimum 2.5 GPA both in SSC/Dakhil and HSC/ Alim. Students from Science group may apply for admission. O level/A level: Students completing five O-level subjects and at least two A-level subjects may apply. Out of these 7 subjects' applicants must have minimum 4 'B' grade 3 'C' grade. The applicants must have Chemistry and Biology both at O-level and A-Level.
intake: Spring: Jan-Apr / Summer: May-Aug / Fall: Sep-Dec
